skill.name: parallel-task
skill.description: Only to be triggered by explicit /parallel-task commands. An orchestration skill that parses plan files and delegates tasks to parallel subagents using task dependencies, launching them in waves until all tasks are completed.
skill.summary: Orchestrate parallel subagent task execution with dependency management
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external_commands and weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but evaluation confirms these are false positives. The 'external_commands' detections are markdown backticks for code formatting (e.g., `### T1:`). The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are markdown bold syntax (e.g., `**depends_on**`). This is a legitimate agent orchestration skill that launches subagents through the Task tool - no shell execution or crypto operations are present.

content.value_statement: This skill enables Claude Code to orchestrate multiple subagents working in parallel on a development plan, managing task dependencies and ensuring correct execution order.
actual_capabilities: Parse markdown plan files to extract task definitions and dependencies
Launch subagents in parallel waves based on dependency resolution
Track task completion and validate results against acceptance criteria
Update plan files with work logs after each task completion
Manage sequential task execution when dependencies block parallel work
Retry failed tasks or escalate issues automatically
limitations: Requires a properly formatted markdown plan file with task definitions
Cannot execute tasks without clear dependency declarations
Validation is limited to what is specified in the plan acceptance criteria
Relies on subagents completing their work correctly - cannot fix broken implementations

use_cases.title: Large Feature Implementation
use_cases.description: Break down a large feature into tasks and implement them in parallel using multiple subagents, managing dependencies automatically.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers working on complex features requiring multiple coordinated changes

use_cases.title: Project Migration
use_cases.description: Parse a migration plan and execute database, API, and frontend changes in parallel waves while maintaining dependency order.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers and architects managing complex migrations

use_cases.title: Multi-File Refactoring
use_cases.description: Coordinate a large-scale refactoring across multiple files with interdependent changes that must happen in specific order.
use_cases.target_user: Senior developers leading team refactoring efforts

prompt_templates.title: Basic Plan Execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the parallel-task skill to execute the plan in my-plan.md
prompt_templates.scenario: Run all tasks from a plan file

prompt_templates.title: Specific Task Subset
prompt_templates.scenario: Run only specific tasks with their dependencies

prompt_templates.title: With Task Filter
prompt_templates.scenario: Run filtered tasks using alternative syntax

prompt_templates.title: Full Orchestration
prompt_templates.prompt: Parse the development-plan.md file, identify all tasks with dependencies, and execute them in parallel waves until complete. Update the plan with logs after each task.

output_examples.output: Launched 3 parallel subagents for tasks T1, T2, T3 (all unblocked). Task T1 completed successfully - created auth-service.ts. Task T2 completed - updated routes.ts. Task T3 failed validation - retrying...
Filtered to tasks T1, T3 with dependency T2. Launching T2 first (required by T1). After T2 completes, launching T1 and T3 in parallel...

best_practices: Define clear dependencies between tasks to enable correct parallel execution
Write specific acceptance criteria for each task to enable validation
Keep tasks atomic and committable to avoid merge conflicts between subagents
anti_patterns: Omitting validation steps in task acceptance criteria
Defining tasks that are too large and span multiple files without clear boundaries

faq.question: What format should the plan file use?
faq.answer: Use markdown with task sections marked with ### T1: or ### Task 1.1: headings. Each task should have depends_on, description, location, acceptance criteria, and validation fields.

faq.question: How are tasks launched in parallel?
faq.answer: Tasks are launched in waves. A wave contains all tasks whose dependencies are satisfied. After a wave completes, the next wave is launched.

faq.question: Can I run only specific tasks?
faq.answer: Yes, you can specify task IDs after the plan file: /parallel-task plan.md T1 T2 T4. Only those tasks and their dependencies will run.

faq.question: What happens if a subagent fails?
faq.answer: The orchestrator will have the agent retry the task or escalate the issue. Failed tasks block dependent tasks until resolved.

faq.question: Does this skill push commits?
faq.answer: No, subagents are instructed to only commit their work, never push. This prevents conflicts with other parallel agents.

faq.question: Can I use this with any Claude Code session?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill works with Claude Code, Codex, and other Claude-based agents that support the Task tool for subagent spawning.
